parameters.content:
### Receive Inbound Webhook

Entry point: listens for POST calls on `/wf05992-job`. Use the TEST url while building, switch callers to the PRODUCTION url after publishing.

Next: Probe Endpoint A

### Probe Endpoint A

GET https://service-a.example.com/healthz — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Next: Combine Probe Results

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Probe Endpoint B

GET https://service-b.example.com/healthz — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Next: Combine Probe Results

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Probe Endpoint C

GET https://service-c.example.com/healthz — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Next: Combine Probe Results

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Combine Probe Results

Rejoins 3 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: All Healthy?

### All Healthy?

Two-way gate on `={{ ($json.statusCode || 200) < 400 }}`. TRUE continues the happy path, FALSE takes the alternate branch.

Ports: ①→ Record Uptime Metric · ②→ Already Alerted? (State Check)

### Record Uptime Metric

googleSheets — `append` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Align Field Names · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Already Alerted? (State Check)

Cross-execution dedupe: remembers keys it has processed before and drops repeats — the idempotency guard.

Next: Page On-Call via Slack

### Page On-Call via Slack

slack — `action` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Align Field Names

Normalizes field names so every source feeds the same downstream schema.

Next: Send Summary to Gmail

### Send Summary to Gmail

gmail — `action` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Gmail

### Escalate Failure to Gmail

gmail — `action` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2

# Alert stakeholders on changes with Gmail 24/7

**Pattern:** Event Notification · **Level:** Core · **Trigger:** Webhook

## What it does
Alert stakeholders on changes with Gmail 24/7. It fires the moment an external system calls its webhook.

## Why it exists
Manual utilities is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Receive Inbound Webhook → Probe Endpoint A → Combine Probe Results → All Healthy? → Record Uptime Metric → Align Field Names → Send Summary to Gmail

## Use it for
- Teams that want to event notification without manual effort
- Anyone automating utilities in productivity
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Removes copy-paste errors
- Has retries and error handling built in
- Saves hours of manual work each week

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; Google OAuth2; Google OAuth2 / service account; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🔐 Security

Never trust inbound payloads: the webhook path is unguessable. Add header auth or IP allow-listing for defense in depth. Secrets live in n8n credentials — never in node parameters.

## 🧹 Data quality

Dedupe/diff steps make re-runs safe: the same input twice will not create duplicate records. If you change the key fields, clear n8n's dedupe memory (node → 'Clear stored keys') before the next run.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your utilities process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
